topic: Performance craft — camera, posing, and reveal pacing
summary: The difference between $500/week and $5K/week at the same viewer count is craft: camera positioning, body language, reveal pacing, and signature moves. Borrowed principles from burlesque (your costume is your choreography) and portrait photography (eye-level or slightly-above lens, rule of thirds, weight-shifted poses). The performer's job is to direct the viewer's eyes, thoughts, and anticipation — the platform is the stage, the tip menu is the setlist, but performance is what gets tipped.
keyPoints: Camera at eye-level or slightly above — low cameras (looking up) are universally unflattering; elevated cameras slim the neck and create intimate 'looking up at' eye line
Lens distance 50–70 cm for headshot framing, further for full-body; face in upper third of frame when close-cropped, center when full-body (rule of thirds)
Direct-to-lens eye contact ~70% of the time during engaged moments — looking away during reveals heightens intimacy, park gaze on lens for emotionally charged moments
Weight-shifted posing (one leg forward, slight S-curve) with bent elbows and knees — stiffness reads uncomfortable, gentle bends read at-ease
Hands are the second-most-watched element after face — use them: trace collarbone, brush hair, frame face; idle hands drop energy
Layer costumes for sequential removal (jacket → blouse → bra → skirt → stockings → lingerie) — more layers = more menu items, more pacing breaks, more anticipation
Glove peel at 30–60 seconds per glove with eye contact maintained — timeless burlesque technique, the slowness is the point
Prop play (feather boa, fan, robe, sheet) conceals AND reveals in alternation — the eye wants what it can't see
Music as metronome: 3-min song gives verse/bridge/chorus pacing breaks; time reveals to the chorus hit
1–3 signature gestures viewers wait for — a specific wink before reveals, a catchphrase for whales, a trademark goodbye. Repetition is the point
Organize menu items into categories: Affection (1–100 tokens), Pose (50–200), Outfit/reveal (100–500), Interaction (varies), Premium (500–2000+)
Price pose items UNDER outfit-reveal items — poses are momentary/reversible, reveals are finite resources
